ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM JUN-04-2013 TO ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION AT JUN-13-2013, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 503. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: CONTINUED TO JUNE 13, 2013 PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST, WHICH IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER WILL BE HEARD FOLLOWING THE HEARING ON PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED
ORDER OVERRULING IN PART AND SUSTAINING IN PART DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT AND GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-06-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S GENERAL DEMURRER AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THIS ACTION IS NOT TIME-BARRED UNDER C.C.P. SECTIONS 340.2 OR 366.1. DEFENDANT'S GENERAL DEMURRER AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON UNCERTAINTY ARE SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS THE OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND THEIR COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE THE FACTS IDENTIFIED IN THEIR REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT SUBSTANTIVELY OPPOSE THIS PORTION OF DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: MILES LENDWAY OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, PLAINTIFFS TERI MATHENY, ET AL.'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL. HEARING HELD. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS NUMBERED 275919 AND 276138 IS GRANTED. BOTH ACTION ARE CONSOLIDATED AS NO. CGC-10-275919 FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL. THE AUGUST 15, 2013 TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE ASSIGNED TO THE SURVIVAL ACTION 275919 BE MAINTAINED SUCH THAT THE ACTIONS CONSOLIDATED AS CGC-10-275919 WILL HAVE A TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE DATE OF AUGUST 15, 2013. ONE ORDER GRANTING CONSOLIDATION WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: MILES LENDWAY OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD. MOTION / Ntc Of Mo & Mo For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Teri Matheny, et al.
Jin Im of COOLEY MANION JONES LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / opposing party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS TERI MATHENY, ET AL.'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate actions numbered 275919 and 276138 is granted. Both action are consolidated as No. CGC-10-275919 for all purposes including trial. The August 15, 2013 Trial Setting Conference assigned to the survival action 275919 be maintained such that the actions consolidated as CGC-10-275919 will have a Trial Setting Conference date of August 15, 2013. One order granting consolidation was signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jin Im of COOLEY MANION JONES LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Teri Matheny, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s demurrer, which was continued from June 4, 2013 to this date per plaintiff's request and Court's order, is denied as moot. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David M. Abner of COOLEY MANION JONES LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party The Boeing Company
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Teri Matheny, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Boeing Company's general demurrer and special demurrers to plaintiffs' first and second causes of action based on the statute of limitations argument are overruled. Plaintiffs' complaint states facts sufficient to demonstrate that this action is not time-barred under C.C.P. sections 340.2 or 366.1. Defendant's general demurrer and special demurrers to plaintiffs' first and second causes of action based on uncertainty are sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity to amend their complaint to allege the facts identified in their request for leave to amend. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose this portion of defendant's demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David M. Abner of COOLEY MANION JONES LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party The Boeing Company
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Teri Matheny, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant The Boeing Company's demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is denied without prejudice as untimely. Defendant's proof of service demonstrates that defendant electronically served its demurrer on April 23, 2013. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and 1010.6. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
